normative_rules: Every M5 depth-surface telemetry or diagnostic signal must bind to a declared schema row before it ships.
Open-source and local builds stay opt-in and local-first for crash, performance, and feature-usage signals.
Managed builds may narrow consent, endpoint, retention, or packet contents, but they may not silently broaden fields beyond the shipped registry vocabulary.
Support bundles reuse the same registry for classification and redaction, but they remain explicit export flows rather than ambient telemetry.

schema_declarations.purpose: Local crash evidence for the depth surface stays on-device until the user or policy explicitly submits it.
Coarse performance summaries stay sampled, redaction-aware, and opt-in only.
Feature-usage counters remain coarse, stable-named, and opt-in only.
Support export remains a reviewed local-first bundle manifest rather than always-on collection.

consent_ledger_bindings.inheritance_note: Depth-surface crash diagnostics inherit the canonical crash-payload consent posture: local capture first, explicit submission later, never automatic upload.

The current repo-wide consent ledger has no dedicated performance family, so M5 performance summaries inherit the strict opt-in local-first telemetry posture while staying narrower than the coarse product telemetry field set.
Depth-surface feature-usage counters inherit the canonical UX telemetry row so coarse capability counters stay opt-in, sampled, and non-coercive on local-only lanes.
Depth-surface support export inherits the canonical support-bundle manifest row: explicit preview and user/admin initiation only, with no always-on telemetry interpretation.

surface_inspections.inspection_note: Notebook kernel exposes crash, performance, usage, and support-export posture with local-only alternatives visible in diagnostics and CLI/headless output.

export_safe_summary: M5 depth surfaces declare crash, performance, feature-usage, and support-export schemas with local-first consent posture, inspectable endpoint truth, and redaction-default packet classes.
